description: Reading a disposed node is an error — the same contract as disposing an effect (#lzspecedgeindex). This holds for a derived slot (`dispose_slot`), a source cell (`dispose_cell`), and transitively for a live reader that still names a disposed node: the reader throws on its next recompute rather than silently observing a stale, default, or recycled value. A binding that returns the last-computed value, a zero value, or `null` instead of erroring is non-conforming, because callers cannot then distinguish 'torn down' from 'legitimately this value', and a use-after-dispose bug surfaces as a wrong number far from its cause. Disposing an already-disposed handle is *not* an error — it is a no-op, so teardown paths stay idempotent — and neither is disposing through a handle whose id has been recycled onto a node of another kind (that too is a no-op; see recycled_id_inherits_nothing.json).

expect.note: reading the disposed slot itself errors
a live reader that still names a disposed dependency errors on its next recompute; it MUST NOT return the value it computed before the disposal
disposing twice is a no-op, not an error — teardown paths must be idempotent
a disposed source cell reads as an error too — dispose_cell and dispose_slot share one contract
